Issue No. 2 · March 2026
March Madness Has Taken Off
We still have one week of releases left and anything is possible. April is positioned to be a high-volume month with a new Super Mario Bros. movie and the Michael Jackson biopic. It's set to be a wild month with some sleepers bound to break out.
Hail Mary Pick Pays Off
Project Hail Mary — now playing
It was a straight steal at the third pick of the month. What an extraordinary comeback for someone who once held a healthy lead in first place. After sitting in the bottom two for a few weeks, Matt has risen back into the top three thanks to the explosive box office of Project Hail Mary. Ryan Gosling and a rock stole the hearts of America this weekend. Not only did it achieve the highest box office for MGM outside of a sequel or franchise, but it also boasts a high "Certified Fresh" RT score of 95%. Matt secured the one-two punch of critical acclaim and a #1 finish. Will he lift off back into that first spot if word-of-mouth keeps totals rising? He still has two movies left to release this month, leaving some major bonuses on the table alongside his box office haul.
Ready or Not? It Has a Ways to Go
Ready or Not 2: Here I Come — now playing
Ready or Not 2: Here I Come was a success for the studio, earning a modest $9M opening weekend and high praise from fans and critics alike. However, the box office total was hindered by a massive overlap in audiences; when the average moviegoer in the Midwest had to choose a film this weekend, Project Hail Mary won out. Perhaps a strong second week will keep it steady? Nonetheless, it's a win for the studio. Jordan still has two movies releasing this month: the beloved re-release of The Mummy Returns and an action flick in a similar vein to Ready or Not. Jordan has a preference, and it shows — let's see how it all pays out.
Keep Hopping Along
Hoppers — now playing
Hoppers continues to thrive for Ben, finishing the weekend in second place. It has been a true workhorse, but will time show that choosing it first overall wasn't the best-case scenario? Project Hail Mary debuted at a total it took Hoppers two weeks to reach. Meanwhile, the wide release of The Pout-Pout Fish was a minor success for the studio but a disappointment for the box office. Despite releasing in over 1,000 theaters, it has reported a minor box office of under $500k so far. As things stand, we should see Ben and at least one other player top $300M by next week, but he could easily be passed.
The Undertone Isn't as Bleak
Undertone — now playing
Nick has a way to go to cross the $200M mark, but he continues to be the premier scout for "Fresh" RT scores. He truly has a sixth sense for quality. These picks are keeping him competitive, and with his selections for next month and the summer coming in hot, he is bound to land plenty more. It's a long game and he's pacing himself nicely; the year is extremely young, and anything can happen.
Reminders of What is Left
Reminders of Him — now playing
Dominic didn't have many options in a shallow month for wide releases, but he was lucky to snag Reminders of Him. The film is trending nicely and punching well above its weight class. Despite the influx of power players released this week, it remains in the top five and is holding strong. While the rest of his roster consisted of very low-earning independent releases, he utilized his resources well to navigate their runs. He is poised for a strong summer.
Looking Ahead: April is positioned to be a high-volume month with a new Super Mario Bros. movie and the Michael Jackson biopic. It's set to be a wild month with some sleepers bound to break out. 🎬
Previous Issues
Issue No. 1 · Bloody Valentine's Day
February 2026
▼
Issue No. 1 · February 2026
Bloody Valentine's Day
The weekend of love has come and gone. The biggest one of the month in terms of wide releases and holidays (Presidents' Day). A few of the players threw all their cards in for this weekend. It paid off to a certain extent. Only time will tell if it was the best-case scenario with the following weeks lacking too many big releases.
Wuthering Heights Brings Ben to New Heights Altogether
Wuthering Heights — now playing
What an absolute turn of events for someone who has been comfortable in the last-place battle with multiple misses since the beginning. The tides have finally turned for once. Wuthering Heights has opened to the biggest opening of 2026 so far with $32.8m. The kicker is the movie has the most reviews on Rotten Tomatoes with over 260! And it is barely hanging on to a fresh score of 60 flat. An absolute nail-biter waiting for the RT boost. Safely lands in 3rd place overall with $86,520,676.
Dom Moving Up to the GOAT
GOAT — now playing
Absolute steal with GOAT overall. The movie is beating the odds and safely netting a high RT score of 80% and barely staying out of 1st place with $27.5m. The kids' movie looks to be heading into a strong 2nd week with word of mouth. Kids' movies like this should have legs. He is safely in 2nd place crossing the $100m threshold with $106,126,057.
Good Luck, Have Fun, and Don't Lose the Lead Matt
Send Help — now playing
Matt continues to dominate the box office with his previous releases. They are holding strong with the new movies coming out. Send Help doesn't need any help at all. It had a less than 1% drop from last week. Unheard of. He banked on 2 movies this week and both have been given the RT bonus to help pad his lead, but they did not make much at the box office to date. Maybe time will tell. He leads with a wide lead of $128,048,596.
Crime 101: RT Pays Nick
Crime 101 — now playing
Nick cannot exactly catch a break; while failing to make too much with the box office itself, CRIME 101 is one of the highest-rated crime thrillers in recent memory. Critics and fans love it. The problem is being released on a lovers' weekend. It opened in 3rd place with $14.3m. Not a bad start, but a movie of this caliber needs to have a spree of killer days to recoup its money. Word of mouth can do it justice. Also, getting a Nirvana movie with a nice little bump in the box office was a great last grab of the draft. Nick might not win off the box office alone, but the strategy of getting the RT bonus is next tier. Every single release of his so far has it. He's at $74,875,799.
The Jordan Milo Mio Experiment Pays Off
Solo Mio — now playing
Milo comes in to help Jordan pass the $10m gross for a release. It's almost double his next highest release. The month is filled with a lot of smaller releases and some of us got caught holding the bag. Jordan and Ben are some of those players. The Mortuary Assistant actually did pretty well for the amount of theaters it was released in. The problem is just that the week was too crowded for another horror movie to break the mold. Still plenty of time left for other releases to come. Scream 7 is looking to slash into that 5th place spot and kill some of the competition to move up the ranks with how it's tracking as of now. This end of the month is going to be killer. He is currently at $51,154,518.
The month isn't over yet. Plenty left to go and plenty to be made. All players have passed the $50m threshold in just February. 🎬
